ops.title: getaway v2: the contract is the CLI; the script is a template
ops.body:
# getaway v2 ruling: the contract is the CLI; the script is a template

Settled with the v2 redesign (2026-07-13). v1 enforced quota gating, injection guards, and sequencing as script-only discipline in plan-trip.js — prose re-derived enforcement, so ad-hoc workflows diverged by design.

## The ruling

`trip compile <slug>` / `trip explain <slug>` ARE the orchestration contract: they emit the node graph — per-node inputs, outputs, freshness/TTL, worst-case quota cost, `requires`, the exact ready-made command line per runnable node, and a model-routing hint `{model, effort}`. Any walker (the shipped `plan-trip.js` or an ad-hoc one) consumes the graph: splice emitted commands verbatim (never interpolate tainted values), preflight `requires` before dispatch, stamp progress per node id, and trust CLI checkpoint state over agent prose — an agent returning with its node unstamped retries once, then surfaces as that node's `failed` state; a null fan-out result feeds the same path, never a dereference.

`plan-trip.js` is the codified reference walker for the canonical ask — well-fought, not mandatory. Non-canonical asks (multi-city, positioning-led, partial refreshes) author their own walker per `references/workflows.md`, which stays thin because the CLI owns the rules: no reproduced regex sets, no dependency tables, no prose quota discipline.

## Routing rides the graph

Mechanical node-runners (emitted-command execution, JSON shaping) → sonnet low; single-fact labeling → haiku; research and judgment (evidence collectors, assess, the stays browser walk) → opus xhigh, with gpt-5.6-terra via the codex-wrapper agent type as an equal research lane. Fable never runs a trip-planning subagent. A walker that ignores the emitted routing burns top-tier tokens on sweep runners — routing is enforced in `agent()` opts, not suggested.
ops.when: Before modifying plan-trip.js, authoring an ad-hoc getaway walker, changing trip compile/explain output, or adjusting model routing for trip-planning agents.
